formula_note: Low uses the lower bound of the police range only. Best uses the police midpoint (700) plus a discounted contribution from the weaker EPR survivor report (+300). High uses police high (800) plus the full EPR report (700) = 1,500.
uncertainty_note: This estimate is conservative. It likely misses some Pakistani army fatalities and may undercount organized-armed deaths if the EPR survivor report is broadly correct.
quality_note: Good direct police range; EPR component weaker and treated cautiously.

formula_note: Derived as residual direct deaths after subtracting military and unknown-status deaths from the all-status Dacca direct-death envelope. Low = 4,000 - 1,500 - 1,000 = 1,500. Best = 5,000 - 1,000 - 750 = 3,250. High = 6,000 - 600 - 500 = 4,900.
uncertainty_note: Residualization stays inside explicit direct violent deaths, but inherits uncertainty from the citywide total and the status split at the university.
quality_note: Reasonably anchored by a strong citywide total, but still indirect because no clean civilian-only Dhaka total was found.

formula_note: Conservative holding category for Dhaka University student deaths because the source range is direct but the status mix is ambiguous. Best is the midpoint of 500-1,000 = 750.
uncertainty_note: If future work can separate armed resisters from noncombatant students, this bucket should shrink and be reallocated.
quality_note: Direct count range exists, but status is genuinely contested.

formula_note: Scenario band for organized-armed personnel physically present or directly engaged in the Dhaka theater over 7 days. Low = 6,000 x 7/30 = 1,400 person-months; Best = 7,500 x 7/30 = 1,750; High = 9,500 x 7/30 ≈ 2,217. The force band reflects four West Pakistani infantry battalions plus support units, alongside EPR/police/other Bengali organized-armed personnel directly in the theater.
uncertainty_note: Source disagreement is substantial: Farman gives 2,500 EPR and 2,000 reserve police, while Salik gives 5,000 EPR and 1,000 police. This estimate widens rather than averages those differences.
quality_note: Force identity is clear; force strength is not, so the estimate is explicitly scenario-based.

formula_note: Backcast March 1971 Dhaka civilian baseline at 1.15m / 1.30m / 1.50m, bounded by 1961 and 1974 city counts and reduced below a naive linear interpolation because the source says post-1971 growth accelerated after Dhaka became the capital. Apply average in-episode outflow of 120k / 75k / 30k via half-outflow adjustment and multiply by 7/30: Low ≈ 254,333; Best ≈ 294,583; High = 346,500 person-months.
uncertainty_note: This is the weakest core input. No direct March 1971 city population or daily outflow series was found, so the denominator is model-generated from historical city counts plus a contemporaneous flight description.
quality_note: Good city-level anchors exist, but the 1971 episode denominator still requires transparent modeling.

formula_note: DR_v1 = (mil_deaths_direct / mil_person_months) / (civ_deaths_direct / civ_person_months). Low pairs low military deaths with high military exposure and high civilian deaths with low civilian exposure. Best uses all best values. High pairs high military deaths with low military exposure and low civilian deaths with high civilian exposure.
uncertainty_note: Best estimate ≈ 52. The interval is intentionally wide (14 to 248). Even if the unknown-status university deaths were all reallocated to civilians, the best-case DR would still remain well above 1 (roughly low-40s).
quality_note: Strong directional signal but broad interval because both denominators are partly reconstructed.
